title: Local Dependence Biclustering — LDB
text: Latent dependence Biclustering, incorporates biclustering Bayesian network model.

title: Local Dependence Biclustering — LDB
code: LDB(   U,   Z = NULL,   w = NULL,   na = NULL,   ncls = 2,   method = "R",   conf = NULL,   g_list = NULL,   adj_list = NULL,   adj_file = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   beta1 = 1,   beta2 = 1 )

title: Local Dependence Biclustering — LDB
text: U U either data class exametrika, raw data. raw data given, converted exametrika class dataFormat function. Z Z missing indicator matrix type matrix data.frame w w item weight vector na na argument specifies numbers characters treated missing values. ncls number latent class(rank). default 2. method specify model analyze data.Local dependence latent class model set "C", latent rank model set "R". default "R". conf confirmatory parameter, can input either vector items corresponding fields sequence, field membership profile matrix. case former, field membership profile matrix generated internally. providing membership profile matrix, needs either matrix data.frame. number fields(nfld) overwrite number columns matrix. g_list list compiling graph-type objects rank/class. adj_list list compiling matrix-type adjacency matrices rank/class. adj_file file detailing relationships graph rank/class, listed order starting point, ending point, rank(class). verbose verbose output Flag. default TRUE beta1 Beta distribution parameter 1 prior density. Default 1. beta2 Beta distribution parameter 2 prior density. Default 1.

title: Local Dependence Biclustering — LDB
text: nobs Sample size. number rows dataset. testlength Length test. number items included test. msg character string indicating model type. Nclass Optimal number classes. Nfield Optimal number fields. crr Correct Response Rate ItemLabel Label Items FieldLabel Label Fields adj_list List Adjacency matrix used model g_list List graph object used model IRP List Estimated Parameters. object three-dimensional PIRP array, dimension represents number rank,number field, Dmax. Dmax denotes maximum number correct response patterns field. LFD Latent Field Distribution. see also plot.exametrika LRD Latent Rank Distribution. see also plot.exametrika FRP Marginal Field Reference Matrix FRPIndex Index FFP includes item location parameters B Beta, slope parameters Alpha, monotonicity indices C Gamma. CCRR_table table rearrangement IRP data.frame format output, consisting combinations rank ,field PIRP. TRP Test Reference Profile RMD Rank Membership Distribution. FieldEstimated Given vector correspondence items fields. ClassEstimated index indicating class student belongs , estimated confirmatory Ranklustering. Students Rank Membership Profile matrix.s-th row vector \(\hat{M}_R\), \(\hat{m}_R\), rank membership profile Student s, namely posterior probability distribution representing student's belonging respective latent classes. also includes rank maximum estimated membership probability, well rank-odds rank-odds. TestFitIndices Overall fit index test.See also TestFit

title: Local Dependence Biclustering — LDB

what: Local Dependence Latent Rank Analysis — LDLRA
title: Local Dependence Latent Rank Analysis — LDLRA
text: performs local dependence latent lank analysis(LD_LRA) Shojima(2011)

title: Local Dependence Latent Rank Analysis — LDLRA
code: LDLRA(   U,   Z = NULL,   w = NULL,   na = NULL,   ncls = 2,   method = "R",   g_list = NULL,   adj_list = NULL,   adj_file = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   beta1 = 2,   beta2 = 2 )

title: Local Dependence Latent Rank Analysis — LDLRA
text: U U either data class exametrika, raw data. raw data given, converted exametrika class dataFormat function. Z Z missing indicator matrix type matrix data.frame w w item weight vector na na argument specifies numbers characters treated missing values. ncls number latent class(rank). default 2. method specify model analyze data.Local dependence latent class model set "C", latent rank model set "R". default "R". g_list list compiling graph-type objects rank/class. adj_list list compiling matrix-type adjacency matrices rank/class. adj_file file detailing relationships graph rank/class, listed order starting point, ending point, rank(class). verbose verbose output Flag. default TRUE beta1 Beta distribution parameter 1 prior density rank reference matrix. Default 2. beta2 Beta distribution parameter 2 prior density rank reference matrix. Default 2.

title: Local Dependence Latent Rank Analysis — LDLRA
text: nobs Sample size. number rows dataset. msg character string indicating model type. testlength Length test. number items included test. crr correct response ratio adj_list adjacency matrix list g_list graph list referenceMatrix Learned Parameters.three-dimensional array patterns item x rank x pattern. IRP Marginal Item Reference Matrix IRPIndex IRP Indices include Alpha, Beta, Gamma. TRP Test Reference Profile matrix. LRD latent Rank/Class Distribution RMD Rank/Class Membership Distribution TestFitIndices Overall fit index test.See also TestFit Estimation_table Estimated parameters tables. CCRR_table Correct Response Rate tables Studens Student information. includes estimated class membership, probability class membership, RUO, RDO.

title: Local Dependence Latent Rank Analysis — LDLRA
text: function intended perform LD-LRA. LD-LRA analysis combines LRA BNM, used analyze network structure among items latent rank. function, structural learning performed, need provide item graphs rank separate files. file format plain text CSV includes edges (, ) rank numbers.

title: Local Dependence Latent Rank Analysis — LDLRA
code: # \donttest{ # Create sample DAG structure with different rank levels # Format: From, To, Rank DAG_dat <- matrix(c(   "From", "To", "Rank",   "Item01", "Item02", "1", # Simple structure for Rank 1   "Item01", "Item02", "2", # More complex structure for Rank 2   "Item02", "Item03", "2",   "Item01", "Item02", "3", # Additional connections for Rank 3   "Item02", "Item03", "3",   "Item03", "Item04", "3" ), ncol = 3, byrow = TRUE)  # Method 1: Directly use graph and adjacency lists g_list <- list() adj_list <- list()  for (i in 1:3) {   adj_R <- DAG_dat[DAG_dat[, 3] == as.character(i), 1:2, drop = FALSE]   g_tmp <- igraph::graph_from_data_frame(     d = data.frame(       From = adj_R[, 1],       To = adj_R[, 2]     ),     directed = TRUE   )   adj_tmp <- igraph::as_adjacency_matrix(g_tmp)   g_list[[i]] <- g_tmp   adj_list[[i]] <- adj_tmp }  # Fit Local Dependence Latent Rank Analysis result.LDLRA1 <- LDLRA(J12S5000,   ncls = 3,   g_list = g_list,   adj_list = adj_list ) #> No ID column detected. All columns treated as response data. Sequential IDs (Student1, Student2, ...) were generated. Use id= parameter to specify the ID column explicitly.  # Plot Item Reference Profiles (IRP) in a 4x3 grid # Shows the probability patterns of correct responses for each item across ranks plot(result.LDLRA1, type = "IRP", nc = 4, nr = 3)   # Plot Test Reference Profile (TRP) # Displays the overall pattern of correct response probabilities across ranks plot(result.LDLRA1, type = "TRP")   # Plot Latent Rank Distribution (LRD) # Shows the distribution of students across different ranks plot(result.LDLRA1, type = "LRD")  # }

what: Structure Learning for LDLRA by PBIL algorithm — LDLRA_PBIL
title: Structure Learning for LDLRA by PBIL algorithm — LDLRA_PBIL
text: Generating DAG list data using Population-Based Incremental learning

title: Structure Learning for LDLRA by PBIL algorithm — LDLRA_PBIL
code: LDLRA_PBIL(   U,   Z = NULL,   w = NULL,   na = NULL,   seed = 123,   ncls = 2,   method = "R",   population = 20,   Rs = 0.5,   Rm = 0.002,   maxParents = 2,   maxGeneration = 100,   successiveLimit = 5,   elitism = 0,   alpha = 0.05,   estimate = 1,   filename = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   beta1 = 2,   beta2 = 2 )

title: Structure Learning for LDLRA by PBIL algorithm — LDLRA_PBIL
text: U U either data class exametrika, raw data. raw data given, converted exametrika class dataFormat function. Z Z missing indicator matrix type matrix data.frame w w item weight vector na na argument specifies numbers characters treated missing values. seed seed random. ncls number latent class(rank). default 2. method specify model analyze data.Local dependence latent class model set "C", latent rank model set "R". default "R". population Population size. default 20 Rs Survival Rate. default 0.5 Rm Mutation Rate. default 0.002 maxParents Maximum number edges emanating single node. default 2. maxGeneration Maximum number generations. successiveLimit Termination conditions. optimal individual change number generations, considered converged. elitism Number elites remain without crossover transitioning next generation. alpha Learning rate. default 0.05 estimate PBIL estimating adjacency matrix, specify number following four methods: 1. Optimal adjacency matrix, 2. Rounded average individuals last generation, 3. Rounded average survivors last generation, 4. Rounded generational gene last generation. default 1. filename Specify filename saving generated adjacency matrix CSV format. default null, output written file. verbose verbose output Flag. default TRUE beta1 Beta distribution parameter 1 prior density. Default 2. beta2 Beta distribution parameter 2 prior density. Default 2.

title: Structure Learning for LDLRA by PBIL algorithm — LDLRA_PBIL
text: nobs Sample size. number rows dataset. testlength Length test. number items included test. crr correct response ratio adj_list adjacency matrix list g_list graph list referenceMatrix Learned Parameters.three-dimensional array patterns item x rank x pattern. IRP Marginal Item Reference Matrix IRPIndex IRP Indices include Alpha, Beta, Gamma. TRP Test Reference Profile matrix. LRD latent Rank/Class Distribution RMD Rank/Class Membership Distribution TestFitIndices Overall fit index test.See also TestFit Estimation_table Estimated parameters tables. CCRR_table Correct Response Rate tables Studens Student information. includes estimated class membership, probability class membership, RUO, RDO.

title: Structure Learning for LDLRA by PBIL algorithm — LDLRA_PBIL
text: function performs structural learning classes using Population-Based Incremental Learning model(PBIL) proposed Fukuda et al.(2014) within genetic algorithm framework. Instead learning adjacency matrix , 'genes genes' generate adjacency matrix updated generation. details, please refer Fukuda(2014) Section 9.4.3 text(Shojima,2022).

title: Structure Learning for LDLRA by PBIL algorithm — LDLRA_PBIL
text: Fukuda, S., Yamanaka, Y., & Yoshihiro, T. (2014). Probability-based evolutionary algorithm mutations learn Bayesian networks. International Journal Artificial Intelligence Interactive Multimedia, 3, 7–13. DOI: 10.9781/ijimai.2014.311

title: Structure Learning for LDLRA by PBIL algorithm — LDLRA_PBIL
code: # \donttest{ # Perform Structure Learning for LDLRA using PBIL algorithm # This process may take considerable time due to evolutionary optimization result.LDLRA.PBIL <- LDLRA_PBIL(J35S515,   seed = 123, # Set random seed for reproducibility   ncls = 5, # Number of latent ranks   maxGeneration = 10,   method = "R", # Use rank model (vs. class model)   elitism = 1, # Keep best solution in each generation   successiveLimit = 15 # Convergence criterion ) #> local dependence latent Rank model is chosen. #> Warning: Too many survivers. title: Latent Rank Analysis — LRA
text: general function estimating Latent Rank Analysis across different response types. function automatically dispatches appropriate method based response type: binary data (LRA.binary): Analysis using either SOM GTM method ordinal data (LRA.ordinal): Analysis using GTM method category thresholds rated data (LRA.rated): Analysis using GTM method rating categories Latent Rank Analysis identifies underlying rank structures test data assigns examinees ranks based response patterns.

title: Latent Rank Analysis — LRA
code: LRA(U, ...)  # Default S3 method LRA(U, na = NULL, Z = NULL, w = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'binary' LRA(   U,   nrank = 2,   method = "GTM",   mic = FALSE,   maxiter = 100,   BIC.check = FALSE,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   beta1 = 1,   beta2 = 1,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'ordinal' LRA(   U,   nrank = 2,   mic = FALSE,   maxiter = 100,   trapezoidal = 0,   eps = 1e-04,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'rated' LRA(   U,   nrank = 2,   mic = FALSE,   maxiter = 100,   trapezoidal = 0,   eps = 1e-04,   minFreqRatio = 0,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Latent Rank Analysis — LRA
text: U Either object class "exametrika" raw data. raw data given, converted exametrika class dataFormat function. ... Additional arguments passed specific methods. na Values treated missing values. Z Missing indicator matrix type matrix data.frame. 1 indicates observed values, 0 indicates missing values. w Item weight vector. nrank Number latent ranks estimate. Must 2 20. method binary data . Either "SOM" (Self-Organizing Maps) "GTM" (Gaussian Topographic Mapping). Default "GTM". mic Logical; TRUE, forces Item Reference Profiles monotonically increasing. Default FALSE. maxiter Maximum number iterations estimation. Default 100. BIC.check binary data SOM method . TRUE, convergence checked using BIC values. Default FALSE. seed binary data SOM method . Random seed reproducibility. verbose Logical; TRUE, displays detailed progress estimation. Default TRUE. beta1 Beta distribution parameter 1 prior density rank reference matrix (GTM method ). Default 1. beta2 Beta distribution parameter 2 prior density rank reference matrix (GTM method ). Default 1. trapezoidal Specifies height tails using trapezoidal prior distribution. Must less 1/nrank. default value 0, results uniform prior distribution. eps Convergence threshold parameter updates. Default 1e-4. minFreqRatio Minimum frequency ratio response categories (default = 0). Categories occurrence rates threshold excluded analysis. example, set 0.1, response categories appear less 10% responses item omitted.

title: Latent Rank Analysis — LRA
text: list class "exametrika" specific subclass (e.g., "LRA", "LRAordinal", "LRArated") containing following common elements: msg character string indicating model type. testlength Length test (number items). nobs Sample size (number rows dataset). Nrank Number latent ranks specified. N_Cycle Number EM algorithm iterations performed. converge Logical value indicating whether algorithm converged within maxiter iterations TRP Test Reference Profile vector showing expected scores rank. LRD Latent Rank Distribution vector showing number examinees rank. RMD Rank Membership Distribution vector showing sum probabilities rank. Students Rank Membership Profile matrix showing posterior probabilities examinees belonging rank, along estimated ranks odds ratios. ItemFitIndices Fit indices item. See also ItemFit. TestFitIndices Overall fit indices test. See also TestFit. subclass returns additional specific elements, detailed respective documentation. binary data (LRA.binary), returned list additionally includes: IRP Item Reference Profile matrix showing probability correct response item across different ranks. IRPIndex Item Response Profile indices including location parameters B Beta, slope parameters Alpha, monotonicity indices C Gamma. ordinal data (LRA.ordinal), returned list additionally includes: msg character string indicating model type. converge Logical value indicating whether algorithm converged within maxiter iterations ScoreReport Descriptive statistics test performance, including sample size, test length, central tendency, variability, distribution characteristics, reliability. ItemReport Basic statistics item including category proportions item-total correlations. ICBR Item Category Boundary Reference matrix showing cumulative probabilities rank-category combinations. ICRP Item Category Reference Profile matrix showing probability response category rank. ScoreRankCorr Spearman's correlation test scores estimated ranks. RankQuantCorr Spearman's correlation estimated ranks quantile groups. ScoreRank Contingency table raw scores estimated ranks. ScoreMembership Expected rank memberships raw score. RankQuantile Cross-tabulation rank frequencies quantile groups. MembQuantile Cross-tabulation rank membership probabilities quantile groups. CatQuant Response patterns across item categories quantile groups. rated data (LRA.rated), returned list additionally includes: msg character string indicating model type. converge Logical value indicating whether algorithm converged within maxiter iterations ScoreReport Descriptive statistics test performance, including sample size, test length, central tendency, variability, distribution characteristics, reliability. ItemReport Basic statistics item including category proportions item-total correlations. ICRP Item Category Reference Profile matrix showing probability response category rank. ScoreRankCorr Spearman's correlation test scores estimated ranks. RankQuantCorr Spearman's correlation estimated ranks quantile groups. ScoreRank Contingency table raw scores estimated ranks. ScoreMembership Expected rank memberships raw score. RankQuantile Cross-tabulation rank frequencies quantile groups. MembQuantile Cross-tabulation rank membership probabilities quantile groups. ItemQuantileRef Reference values item across quantile groups. CatQuant Response patterns across item categories quantile groups.

title: Latent Rank Analysis — LRA
text: LRA.binary analyzes dichotomous (0/1) response data using either Self-Organizing Maps (SOM) Gaussian Topographic Mapping (GTM).

title: Latent Rank Analysis — LRA
text: LRA.ordinal analyzes ordered categorical data multiple thresholds, Likert-scale responses graded items.

title: Latent Rank Analysis — LRA
text: LRA.rated analyzes data ratings assigned response, partially-credited items preference scales response categories different weights.

title: Latent Rank Analysis — LRA
code: # \donttest{ # Binary data example # Fit a Latent Rank Analysis model with 6 ranks to binary data result.LRA <- LRA(J15S500, nrank = 6)  # Display the first few rows of student rank membership profiles head(result.LRA$Students) #>            Membership 1 Membership 2 Membership 3 Membership 4 Membership 5 #> Student001 0.2704649921  0.357479353   0.27632327  0.084988078  0.010069050 #> Student002 0.0276546965  0.157616072   0.47438958  0.279914853  0.053715813 #> Student003 0.0228189795  0.138860955   0.37884545  0.284817610  0.120794858 #> Student004 0.0020140858  0.015608542   0.09629429  0.216973334  0.362406292 #> Student005 0.5582996437  0.397431414   0.03841668  0.003365601  0.001443909 #> Student006 0.0003866603  0.003168853   0.04801344  0.248329964  0.428747502 #>            Membership 6 Estimate Rank-Up Odds Rank-Down Odds #> Student001 0.0006752546        2    0.7729769      0.7565891 #> Student002 0.0067089816        3    0.5900527      0.3322503 #> Student003 0.0538621490        3    0.7518042      0.3665372 #> Student004 0.3067034562        5    0.8462973      0.5987019 #> Student005 0.0010427491        1    0.7118604             NA #> Student006 0.2713535842        5    0.6328983      0.5791986  # Plot Item Reference Profiles (IRP) for the first 6 items plot(result.LRA, type = "IRP", items = 1:6, nc = 2, nr = 3)   # Plot Test Reference Profile (TRP) showing expected scores at each rank plot(result.LRA, type = "TRP")  # }  # \donttest{ # Ordinal data example # Fit a Latent Rank Analysis model with 3 ranks to ordinal data result.LRAord <- LRA(J15S3810, nrank = 3, mic = TRUE)  # Plot score distributions plot(result.LRAord, type = "ScoreFreq")  plot(result.LRAord, type = "ScoreRank")   # Plot category response patterns for items 1-6 plot(result.LRAord, type = "ICBR", items = 1:6, nc = 3, nr = 2)  plot(result.LRAord, type = "ICRP", items = 1:6, nc = 3, nr = 2)  # }  # \donttest{ # Rated data example # Fit a Latent Rank Analysis model with 10 ranks to rated data result.LRArated <- LRA(J35S5000, nrank = 10, mic = TRUE)  # Plot score distributions plot(result.LRArated, type = "ScoreFreq")  plot(result.LRArated, type = "ScoreRank")   # Plot category response patterns for items 1-6 plot(result.LRArated, type = "ICRP", items = 1:6, nc = 3, nr = 2)  # }

title: Four-Parameter Logistic Model — LogisticModel
text: four-parameter logistic model model one additional parameter d, called upper asymptote parameter, added 3PLM.

title: Four-Parameter Logistic Model — LogisticModel
code: LogisticModel(a = 1, b, c = 0, d = 1, theta)

title: Four-Parameter Logistic Model — LogisticModel
text: slope parameter b location parameter c lower asymptote parameter d upper asymptote parameter theta ability parameter

title: Four-Parameter Logistic Model — LogisticModel
text: Returns numeric vector probabilities c d, representing probability correct response given ability level theta. probability calculated using formula: \(P(\theta) = c + \frac{(d-c)}{1 + e^{-(\theta-b)}}\)

title: Mutual Information — MutualInformation
text: Mutual Information measure represents degree interdependence two items. function applicable binary polytomous response data. measure calculated using joint probability distribution responses item pairs marginal probabilities.

title: Mutual Information — MutualInformation
code: MutualInformation(U, na = NULL, Z = NULL, w = NULL, base = 2)  # Default S3 method MutualInformation(U, na = NULL, Z = NULL, w = NULL, base = 2)  # S3 method for class 'binary' MutualInformation(U, na = NULL, Z = NULL, w = NULL, base = 2)  # S3 method for class 'ordinal' MutualInformation(U, na = NULL, Z = NULL, w = NULL, base = 2)

title: Mutual Information — MutualInformation
text: U Either object class "exametrika" raw data. raw data given, converted exametrika class dataFormat function. na Values treated missing values. Z Missing indicator matrix type matrix data.frame. Values 1 indicate observed responses, 0 indicates missing data. w Item weight vector specifying relative importance item. base base logarithm. Default 2. polytomous data, can use "V" set base min(rows, columns), "e" natural logarithm (base e), number use specific base.

title: Mutual Information — MutualInformation
text: matrix mutual information values exametrika class. element (,j) represents mutual information items j, measured bits. Higher values indicate stronger interdependence items.

title: Mutual Information — MutualInformation
text: binary data, following formula used: $$ MI_{jk} = p_{00} \log_2 \frac{p_{00}}{(1-p_j)(1-p_k)} + p_{01} \log_2 \frac{p_{01}}{(1-p_j)p_k}  + p_{10} \log_2 \frac{p_{10}}{p_j(1-p_k)} + p_{11} \log_2 \frac{p_{11}}{p_jp_k} $$ : \(p_{00}\) joint probability incorrect responses items j k \(p_{01}\) joint probability incorrect response item j correct item k \(p_{10}\) joint probability correct response item j incorrect item k \(p_{11}\) joint probability correct responses items j k polytomous data, following formula used: $$MI_{jk} = \sum_{j=1}^{C_j}\sum_{k=1}^{C_k}p_{jk}\log \frac{p_{jk}}{p_{j.}p_{.k}}$$ base logarithm can number rows, number columns, min(rows, columns), base-10 logarithm, natural logarithm (e), etc.

title: Mutual Information — MutualInformation
text: function implemented using binary data compatibility wrapper raise error used polytomous data.

title: Simple Test Statistics — TestStatistics
text: returned object depends data type: binary data, list class c("exametrika", "TestStatistics") containing: TestLength Length test. number items included test. SampleSize Sample size. number rows dataset. Mean Average number correct answers. SEofMean Standard error mean. Variance Variance test scores. SD Standard Deviation test scores. Skewness Skewness score distribution (measure asymmetry). Kurtosis Kurtosis score distribution (measure tail extremity). Min Minimum score. Max Maximum score. Range Range scores (Max - Min). Q1 First quartile. 25th percentile. Median Median. 50th percentile. Q3 Third quartile. 75th percentile. IQR Interquartile range. Calculated subtracting Q1 Q3. Stanine Stanine score boundaries, see stanine. ordinal rated data, function calls ScoreReport returns result. See ScoreReport details returned object. nominal data, error returned function support nominal data.

title: Simple Test Statistics — TestStatistics
code: # Basic usage stats <- TestStatistics(J15S500) print(stats) #> Test Statistics #>                   value #> TestLength   15.0000000 #> SampleSize  500.0000000 #> Mean          9.6640000 #> SEofMean      0.1190738 #> Variance      7.0892826 #> SD            2.6625707 #> Skewness     -0.4116220 #> Kurtosis     -0.4471624 #> Min           2.0000000 #> Max          15.0000000 #> Range        13.0000000 #> Q1.25%        8.0000000 #> Median.50%   10.0000000 #> Q3.75%       12.0000000 #> IQR           4.0000000 #> Stanine.4%    5.0000000 #> Stanine.11%   6.0000000 #> Stanine.23%   7.0000000 #> Stanine.40%   9.0000000 #> Stanine.60%  11.0000000 #> Stanine.77%  12.0000000 #> Stanine.89%  13.0000000 #> Stanine.96%  14.0000000  # Extract specific statistics cat("Mean score:", stats$Mean, "\n") #> Mean score: 9.664  cat("Standard deviation:", stats$SD, "\n") #> Standard deviation: 2.662571   # View score distribution summary summary_stats <- data.frame(   Min = stats$Min,   Q1 = stats$Q1,   Median = stats$Median,   Mean = stats$Mean,   Q3 = stats$Q3,   Max = stats$Max ) print(summary_stats) #>     Min Q1 Median  Mean Q3 Max #> 25%   2  8     10 9.664 12  15

what: Plot Method for Objects of Class
title: Plot Method for Objects of Class
text: Creates visualizations objects class "exametrika". calculation results exametrika package exametrika class attribute, along specific analysis model class (IRT, GRM, LCA, LRA, Biclustering, Biclustering_IRM, LDLRA, LDB, BINET). model compatible plot types, accessible specifying 'type' parameter.

title: Plot Method for Objects of Class
code: # S3 method for class 'exametrika' plot(   x,   type = c("IRF", "TRF", "IIF", "TIF", "IIC", "ICC", "TIC", "IRP", "TRP", "LCD", "CMP",     "FRP", "RMP", "LRD", "Array", "CRV", "RRV", "FCRP", "FCBR", "ScoreField",     "FieldPIRP", "LDPSR", "ScoreFreq", "ScoreRank", "ICRP", "ICBR"),   items = NULL,   students = NULL,   nc = 1,   nr = 1,   overlay = FALSE,   colors = NULL,   cell_width = 3,   cell_height = 1,   filename = NULL,   width = 800,   height = 600,   dpi = 300,   stat = "mean",   style = "line",   ... )

title: Plot Method for Objects of Class
text: x object class "exametrika" type Character string specifying plot type. Available types vary model: IRF, ICC Item Response Function. Also known 'ICC' (Item Characteristic Curve). TRF Test Response Function. IIF, IIC Item Information Function. Also known 'IIC' (Item Information Curve). TIF, TIC Test Information Function. Also known 'TIC' (Test Information Curve). IRP Item Reference Profile. Line graph items latent classes/ranks horizontal axis, membership probability vertical axis. CRV, RRV Class/Rank Reference Vector. Plots correct answer rates class rank, fields horizontal axis correct answer rates vertical axis. TRP Test Reference Profile. Shows latent classes/ranks horizontal axis, displaying members per class/rank bar graph expected test scores line graph. LCD Latent Class Distribution. Displays latent classes horizontal axis, showing members per class bar graph cumulative membership probability line. LRD Latent Rank Distribution. Similar LCD ranks instead classes horizontal axis. CMP Class Membership Profile. Line graph showing class membership probabilities students. RMP Rank Membership Profile. Similar CMP ranks instead classes. ScoreFreq Frequency polygon score distribution rank thresholds. ScoreRank Heatmap score membership probabilities rank. ICRP Visualizes ranks (x-axis) versus category response probabilities (y-axis). ICBR Visualizes ranks (x-axis) versus cumulative category probabilities (y-axis). FRP Field Reference Profile. Shows correspondence fields latent classes/ranks. Array Array plot Biclustering/Ranklustering. Colored matrix cells darker cells indicate larger values. FieldPIRP Shows correct response rates number correct answers parent fields. available LDB model. LDPSR Latent Dependence Passing Student Rate. Compares passing rates parent child classes. FCRP Field Category Response Profile. Category probability plot per field polytomous Biclustering. Use style parameter choose "line" "bar" display. FCBR Field Cumulative Boundary Reference. Boundary probability plot per field (ordinal Biclustering ). ScoreField Heatmap expected scores across fields latent classes/ranks polytomous Biclustering. items Numeric vector specifying items plot. NULL, items included. type "IIF"/"IIC", specifying 0 produce TIF/TIC entire test. students Numeric vector specifying students plot. NULL, students included. nc Integer specifying number columns multiple plots. Default 1. nr Integer specifying number rows multiple plots. Default 1. overlay Logical. TRUE, elements IRFs overlaid single plot. Default FALSE. colors Character vector specifying custom color palette. NULL, default colorblind-friendly palette used. array plots, first color missing values, followed response category colors. cell_width Numeric value specifying width cell array plots. Default 3. cell_height Numeric value specifying height cell array plots. Default 1. filename Character string specifying output filename. NULL, plot displayed screen. Supported formats: png, pdf, jpeg, jpg. Format determined file extension. width Numeric value specifying plot width pixels (png/jpeg) inches (pdf). Default 800. height Numeric value specifying plot height pixels (png/jpeg) inches (pdf). Default 600. dpi Numeric value specifying resolution dots per inch raster formats (png/jpeg). Default 300. stat Character string specifying summary statistic polytomous FRP RRV plots. One "mean" (default), "median", "mode". style Character string specifying display style FCRP plots. One "line" (default) "bar" (stacked bar chart). ... Additional arguments passed plotting functions.

title: Plot Method for Objects of Class
text: Produces visualizations based model class specified type: IRT models IRF (Item Response Function), TRF (Test Response Function), IIF (Item Information Function), TIF (Test Information Function) LCA/LRA models IRP (Item Reference Profile), TRP (Test Reference Profile), LCD/LRD (Latent Class/Rank Distribution), CMP/RMP (Class/Rank Membership Profile) Biclustering/Biclustering_IRM models Array plots showing clustering patterns, FRP, TRP, etc. LDLRA/LDB/BINET models Network profile plots specific model

title: Plot Method for Objects of Class
text: model class supports specific plot types: IRT Supports "IRF"/"ICC", "TRF", "IIF"/"IIC", "TIF"/"TIC" GRM Supports "IRF"/"ICC", "IIF"/"IIC", "TIF"/"TIC" LCA Supports "IRP", "FRP", "TRP", "LCD", "CMP" LRA Supports "IRP", "FRP", "TRP", "LRD", "RMP" LRAordinal Supports "ScoreFreq", "ScoreRank", "ICRP", "ICBR", "RMP" LRArated Supports "ScoreFreq", "ScoreRank", "ICRP", "RMP" Biclustering Supports "FRP", "TRP", "LCD", "LRD", "CMP", "RMP", "CRV", "RRV", "Array" ordinalBiclustering Supports "FRP", "FCRP", "FCBR", "LCD", "LRD", "CMP", "RMP", "Array", "ScoreField", "RRV" nominalBiclustering Supports "FRP", "FCRP", "LCD", "LRD", "CMP", "Array", "ScoreField", "RRV" Biclustering_IRM Supports "FRP", "TRP", "Array" LDLRA Supports "IRP", "TRP", "LRD", "RMP" LDB Supports "FRP", "TRP", "LRD", "RMP", "Array", "FieldPIRP" BINET Supports "FRP", "TRP", "LRD", "RMP", "Array", "LDPSR"

title: Stanine Scores — stanine
text: Stanine (Standard Nine) scoring system divides students nine groups based normalized distribution. function applicable binary response data. groups correspond following percentile ranges: Stanine 1: lowest 4% (percentiles 1-4) Stanine 2: next 7% (percentiles 5-11) Stanine 3: next 12% (percentiles 12-23) Stanine 4: next 17% (percentiles 24-40) Stanine 5: middle 20% (percentiles 41-60) Stanine 6: next 17% (percentiles 61-77) Stanine 7: next 12% (percentiles 78-89) Stanine 8: next 7% (percentiles 90-96) Stanine 9: highest 4% (percentiles 97-100)

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Fixed CAIC formula match Bozdogan (1987) original definition: CAIC penalty term log(n + 1) log(n) + 1 per Bozdogan (1987, Psychometrika, 52(3), p.358, Proposition 2, Eq.44). original Mathematica implementation error (Log[nobs + 1]), R port inherited . R version (R/00_ModelFitModule.R) Mathematica version (develop/mtmk15forVer13/mod/Module_ModelFit.nb) corrected. numerical difference approximately 1 (constant), corrected formula now matches published definition: CAIC(k) = -2 log L + k * (log(n) + 1). affects models compute fit indices: IRT, LCA, LRA (binary/ordinal/rated), Biclustering (binary/ordinal/nominal), IRM, BNM, LDLRA, LDB, BINET, GRM. 680 tests pass corrected formula.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Index alias support: GridSearch() now accepts common aliases fit indices. "loglik", "log_lik", "LogLik", "LL" mapped "model_log_like". "Chi_sq" "chi_sq" mapped "model_Chi_sq". Previously, using aliases caused silent NULL extraction eventual errors. Early validation: Invalid index names now caught immediately start GridSearch(), computationally expensive grid search loop runs. error message lists valid options including available aliases. Log-likelihood optimization direction: model_log_like now correctly treated maximization target (larger log-likelihood = better fit). Previously, incorrectly placed minimization group, selected worst-fitting model.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Q3 matrix test: Changed 2PL Q3 matrix test relative tolerance (tolerance = 1e-2 expect_equal) absolute difference comparison (threshold 0.005). Q3 residual correlations include values near zero relative tolerance comparisons unreliable.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Missing imports: Added layout plot.new graphics NAMESPACE. functions used legend strip layout helpers polytomous Biclustering plots.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Fixed apply(U$Q, 2, unique) returning matrix instead list: items number response categories (e.g., 5-point Likert), apply() returns matrix rather list. subsequent lapply() iterates individual elements instead per-column vectors, causing ncat vector 1s crashing algorithm. Replaced lapply(seq_len(nitems), function(j) sort(unique(U$Q[, j]))) always returns proper list. fix applied catfreq999 computation. LRA.ordinal LRA.rated affected.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Fixed apply(tmp$Q, 2, table) returning matrix instead list: class bug LRA.ordinal/LRA.rated fix . GRM(), null model log-likelihood computation used apply(tmp$Q, 2, table) compute per-item category frequencies. items number response categories (e.g., 5-point Likert), apply() returns matrix instead list, causing response_list[[j]] extract single number rather full frequency table. produced incorrect null_log_like values consequently wrong chi-square statistics, RMSEA, TLI, CFI ItemFitIndices. Replaced lapply(seq_len(nitems), function(j) table(tmp$Q[, j])) always returns proper list.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Added input validation mixed category counts: LRA.ordinal() now raises informative error items different numbers response categories (e.g., items 3 categories others 5). internal matrix algebra uses fixed-stride indexing assumes uniform category counts. error message suggests alternatives (LRA.rated, Biclustering.ordinal) support mixed category counts via list-based designs.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Changed GRM examples \donttest \dontrun: GRM’s multi-panel plot examples caused “invalid graphics state” errors non-interactive environments (pkgdown, CI). Changed \dontrun prevent build failures.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Removed “FRP” valid plot types LCA LRA: Field Reference Profile (FRP) requires field structure (item grouping), LCA LRA . Previously, plot(lca_result, type = "FRP") passed validation failed runtime $FRP field exist LCA/LRA return values. Now properly rejected informative error message validation stage.

what: dataFormat Rated Response Type Detection Fix
title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Fixed auto-detection ignoring CA parameter: CA (correct answer vector) provided response.type explicitly specified, dataFormat() incorrectly classified data "ordinal" instead "rated". caused $U (binary scoring matrix) NULL. auto-detection now checks CA ordinal/nominal detection: binary → rated (CA provided) → ordinal → nominal. Fixed id parameter working column 1: Changed id default 1 NULL. Previously, id = 1 (default explicit) triggered auto-detection heuristics, making impossible explicitly specify first column ID column. Now id = NULL triggers auto-detection, numeric value (including 1) forces column used ID. Improved ID auto-detection consecutive integers: Simplified first-column heuristic treat unique consecutive integers (e.g., 1:N) ID regardless whether also look like valid response values. Previously, 1:10 misclassified response data looks_like_response_data() returned TRUE. Fixed missing values rated U matrix: response matrix contained missing values (-1), binary scoring matrix U incorrectly scored 0 (incorrect) instead -1 (missing). Now U[,j] = -1 Q[,j] = -1. Fixed drop=FALSE missing item exclusion: items excluded due invalid variance (e.g., containing Inf), column subsetting response.matrix[, mask] dropped matrix dimensions one item remained, causing crash. Added drop = FALSE. Items missing values Items zero variance (constant response values) Students missing responses

what: BINET g_list / adj_list Input Path Fix
title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Fixed g_csv variable undefined error using g_list adj_list input: BINET() crashed undefined variable error graph construction step DAG specified via g_list adj_list parameters. internal variable g_csv (used build integrated graph object all_g) defined adj_file code path. Added logic reconstruct g_csv adj_list g_list adj_list input paths, ensuring all_g correctly built Field edge attributes regardless input method. Fixed g_list / adj_list length validation: length check g_list adj_list incorrectly compared ncls (number classes) instead nfld (number fields). BINET, element adj_list represents DAG structure specific field, list length equal nfld. Also fixed g_list type check g_list[[1]] (always checking first element) g_list[[j]] (checking element).

what: New Sample Datasets for Polytomous Biclustering
title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: J35S500: Simulated ordinal dataset (500 students, 35 items, 5 categories) cumulative staircase pattern (5 latent classes, 5 fields). Contains approximately 0.5% missing values. J20S600: Simulated nominal dataset (600 students, 20 items, 4 categories) cyclic category preference pattern (5 latent classes, 4 fields). Contains approximately 0.5% missing values.

what: New Plot Types for Polytomous Biclustering
title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: FRP (Field Reference Profile): Expected score line plot per field, stat parameter supporting "mean" (default), "median", "mode". FCRP (Field Category Response Profile): Category probability plot per field, style parameter supporting "line" (default) "bar" (stacked bar chart). FCBR (Field Cumulative Boundary Reference): Boundary probability plot per field (ordinal Biclustering ). ScoreField: Heatmap expected scores across fields latent classes/ranks. RRV (Rank Reference Vector): Transposed view fields x-axis expected scores y-axis, stat parameter.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Ordinal Biclustering now computes FRPIndex (Field Reference Profile indices) including location parameters (Alpha, Beta), slope parameters (, B), monotonicity indices (Gamma, C).

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: stat: Controls summary statistic FRP RRV plots ("mean", "median", "mode"). style: Controls display style FCRP plots ("line", "bar").

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Missing value display: Array plots now display missing values distinct color. Binary data uses gray (#808080) distinguish white (incorrect) black (correct). Polytomous data uses black (#000000) distinguish category color palette.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Ordinal Biclustering: print() now displays FRPIndex (Field Reference Profile Indices) note values based normalized expected scores (E[score]-1)/(maxQ-1).

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: FRPIndex: Expanded documentation 6 profile shape indices (Alpha, , Beta, B, Gamma, C) ?Biclustering, including detailed definitions polytomous adaptation logic.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Systematic unification return value structures across analysis functions consistency interoperability.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Biclustering_IRM: Added FRPIndex (Field Reference Profile indices) consistency Biclustering.binary LDB. LDB: Fixed TRP matrix(1×ncls) numeric vector, consistent functions. GridSearch: Added class = c("exametrika", "GridSearch") return value method dispatch support.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: LCA msg field assignment: Fixed msg <- "Class" msg = "Class" inside structure() call (line 150 05_LCA.R). <- operator interpreted standalone assignment rather named list element, causing msg field name empty. RMP/CMP single student plot error: Fixed dimension drop error plotting RMP CMP single student (e.g., plot(r, type="RMP", students=1)). Added drop = FALSE prevent matrix--vector coercion extracting single row Students matrix. LRA.ordinal / LRA.rated TestFitIndices log-likelihood fields: Fixed null_log_like incorrectly stored saturated model log-likelihood (log_lik_satu) instead null model log-likelihood (sum(null_itemll)). Added missing bench_log_like field containing saturated model log-likelihood (sum(satu_itemll2)). Note: chi-square-based fit indices (NFI, CFI, TLI, RMSEA, AIC, BIC, etc.) always computed correctly; stored log-likelihood labels affected. LRA.ordinal / LRA.rated FitIndices structure: Unified TestFitIndices ItemFitIndices standard 16-field structure ModelFit class (c("exametrika", "ModelFit")), matching analysis functions. Added model_log_like, bench_log_like, null_log_like ItemFitIndices. Removed ScoreRankCorr / RankQuantCorr TestFitIndices (already available top level return object). LRA.ordinal / LRA.rated test updates: Updated test-12OLR.R test-13NLR.R handle new ModelFit class (add unclass() .data.frame()) adjusted column/index references match unified 16-field structure.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Legend strip layout: Moved per-panel legends shared legend strip plot area FCRP (line/bar), FCBR, GRM IRF, IRT overlay (IRF/IIF) plots. Uses layout() thin dedicated row (height ratio 0.2) reduce visual clutter data panels. Added setup_legend_layout() draw_legend_strip() internal helper functions. FCBR reference line: Added P(Q>=1)=1.0 reference line top FCBR plots visual completeness.

title: exametrika 1.9.0
text: Complete migration Excel CSV fixtures: Removed 14 legacy test files depended tidyverse readxl reading Excel-based Mathematica reference data. Replaced 23 modern test files using base R read.csv() test_path() load CSV fixtures tests/testthat/fixtures/mathematica_reference/. new test suite zero external package dependencies beyond testthat. Removed readxl/tidyverse test dependencies: DESCRIPTION Suggests longer requires packages beyond knitr, rmarkdown, testthat. Fixture file reorganization: Shortened overly long CSV fixture filenames comply CRAN’s 100-byte portable path requirement. Test coverage: 23 test files covering models (CTT, IRT 2PL/3PL/4PL, LCA, LRA binary/ordinal/nominal, Biclustering binary/ordinal/nominal, IRM, BNM, LDLRA, LDB, BINET, GRM, GridSearch, dataFormat, polychoric correlation, scoring, student/test analysis). 85 Mathematica reference CSV files cross-validation.

CI/CD: Added GitHub Actions workflows automated R CMD check (R-CMD-check.yaml) test coverage reporting (test-coverage.yaml). .Rbuildignore cleanup: Removed ^tests$ ^inst$ entries incorrectly excluding tests vignettes built package.

title: exametrika 1.6.3
text: CRAN release: 2025-09-30 Replaced R implementation high-performance C++ code using Rcpp Implemented analytical gradient computation significant speed improvements Achieved 5-6x faster convergence compared numerical differentiation Maintains identical mathematical accuracy previous implementation Full compatibility existing GRM() function interface Functions affected: Biclustering(), LCA(), LRA(), related methods Returns TRUE converged within maxiter iterations, FALSE otherwise Displays convergence warning messages maxiter reached Automatically excludes non-converged results optimization Displays warning messages parameter combinations failed converge Returns list failed settings output Terminates error message parameter combinations fail converge Implemented conditional pmax() application avoid unnecessary log-likelihood inflation Applied numerical correction NaN/Inf values detected Implemented C++ acceleration polychoric correlation calculations Achieved significant speed improvements R-based implementation Replaced dull default colors vibrant, high-contrast color palette Added colorblind-friendly color scheme better accessibility Binary data (2 categories) now uses black white optimal contrast Multi-category data uses enhanced colorblind-accessible palette
